description: Default port used for connections to vCenter Server. Note: The backup server should have a direct connection to vCenter Server. HTTP/HTTPS proxy servers are not supported. If you use VMware Cloud Director, make sure you open port 443 on underlying vCenter Servers.
Default port used for connections to ESXi host. This port is not required for VMware Cloud on AWS.
Port used for data transfer to ESXi host. It is also used during guest OS file recovery if you recover files from replicas. This port is not required for VMware Cloud on AWS.
Default port used for connections to VMware Cloud Director. Note: The backup server should have a direct connection to VMware Cloud Director. HTTP/HTTPS proxy servers are not supported.

targetService: PostgreSQL server hosting the Veeam Backup & Replication configuration database

targetService: Microsoft SQL Server hosting the Veeam Backup & Replication configuration database

targetService: DNS server with forward/reverse name resolution of all backup servers
description: Port used for communication with the DNS Server.

Default port used to download information about available updates from the Veeam Update Notification Server over HTTPS. Veeam Update Notification Server endpoints: dev.veeam.com vbrad.butler.veeam.com vbrce.butler.veeam.com

Default port used to automatically update license from the Veeam License Update Server over HTTPS. Veeam Threat Hunter and Veeam Data Cloud Vault also require this communication to work properly. Veeam License Update Server endpoints: vbr.butler.veeam.com autolk.veeam.com

Required for certificate validation when Veeam Backup & Replication connects to Veeam License Update Server to check if the new license is available and download it. Certificate verification endpoints: *.ss2.us *.amazontrust.com Consider that certificate verification endpoints (CRL URLs and OCSP servers) are subject to change. You can find the actual list of addresses in the certificate details in the following fields: CRL Distribution Points Authority Information Access

Veeam Backup & Replication requires access to the Certificate Revocation Lists (CRL) of the Certificate Authority (CA) that issued the certificate for each backup infrastructure component. Note : The specific CRL endpoint that must be connected to depends on the CA that issued the certificate. You can find the actual list of addresses in the certificate details in the following fields: CRL Distribution Points Authority Information Access
Default port used for communication with Key Management System server.
Default port used for communication with Veeam ONE internal Web API. Required for the Analytics view. For more information, see Configuring Analytics View.
Default port used by Veeam ONE Web Services. Required for the Analytics view. For more information, see Configuring Analytics View.

Port used locally on the backup server for communication between Veeam Broker Service and Veeam services and components. Note : Local ports do not require specific firewall rules. Make sure that this port is not used by another software. Otherwise, this can affect Veeam Backup & Replication functionality.

Port used to provide REST access to the Veeam Backup & Replication database. Note : Local ports do not require specific firewall rules. Make sure that this port is not used by another software. Otherwise, this can affect Veeam Backup & Replication functionality.

Default port used by the Veeam Guest Catalog service for catalog replication. Can be customized during Veeam Backup & Replication installation. If you have upgraded from an earlier version of the product, the range of ports from 2500 to 5000 applies to the already added components.

Dynamic RPC port range for Microsoft Windows 2008 and later. For more information, see this Microsoft KB article . Note: If you use default Microsoft Windows firewall settings, you do not need to configure dynamic RPC ports. During setup, Veeam Backup & Replication automatically creates a firewall rule for the runtime process. If you use firewall settings other than default ones or application-aware processing fails with the "RPC function call failed" error, you need to configure dynamic RPC ports. For more information on how to configure RPC dynamic port allocation to work with firewalls, see this Microsoft KB article.

targetService: Azure restore proxy appliance (former Azure proxy)
description: Default management and data transport port required for communication with the Azure restore proxy appliance. For details, see Specify Credentials and Transport Port.
